summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a strong ’87 show with standout performances of Black Peter, Peggy‑O and Row Jimmy.
review: The recording is praised for its clarity and solid mix, delivering a noticeably crisper board source than other versions. Musically the concert shines thanks to emotive renditions of Black Peter, a booming Scarlet > Fire jam, and tight takes on Peggy‑O and Row Jimmy, while Jerry’s post‑coma vocal depth adds an unexpected poignancy. A few reviewers note dated keyboards and a waning vocal tone, but overall the show is regarded as one of the better 1987 outings.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high marks for both performance and sound quality across many detailed reviews; weighted average yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the mix crisp, clear and well‑balanced; no major dropouts or distortion reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe the setlist as well‑chosen and consistently strong, with emotional peaks in the second set (Black Peter) and energetic jams (Scarlet > Fire).

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed – some note his voice sounding weakened after his coma, but others praise his deep, emotive delivery on Black Muddy River and overall strong lead work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Positive – bass described as booming, especially during Scarlet > Fire, enough to “make you piss yourself.”
band_member_comments.Bob: Positive – noted for solid rhythm guitar and vocal contributions, “good lovin’” on the night.
band_member_comments.Keys: Mixed – a few call the keyboards "tinkly" and annoying, while others say there’s no tinkly annoyance here.
band_member_comments.Drums: Positive – drums hailed as legendary for 1987, with both Bill and Mickey receiving praise.
